message:
chore(gitignore): ignore .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock and .ntm/logs/*.log runtime ephemerals

Two runtime artifacts kept showing up as dirty across sessions even
though they're purely machine-local:

- .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock: Claude Code's lockfile for the /schedule
  skill. JSON-formatted lockfile that is recreated/touched whenever the
  scheduler runs — not meaningful to version.
- .ntm/logs/*.log: runtime log output appended by NTM agent panes
  (am-beads_rust.log, cm-beads_rust.log, etc.). Already covered by
  ~/.gitignore globally, but also repeated here so the files stay
  ignored on fresh clones or on machines without the global entry.

Pattern-matching the logs rather than the whole .ntm/ directory keeps
any future non-log artifacts under .ntm/ (e.g. config, state files)
trackable if they show up later.

Historically tracked copies of these files still show in `git
ls-files`; they can be retired separately with
`git rm --cached .ntm/logs/*.log` once other agents on this machine
stop writing to them.

files.patch:
@@ -154,3 +154,11 @@ report.json
 .codex-local-check/
 .codex-bench/
 .codex-tmp/
+
+# Claude Code runtime lockfile for the /schedule skill
+.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock
+
+# NTM agent pane runtime logs (also covered globally in ~/.gitignore,
+# but repeat here so they stay ignored on fresh clones or machines
+# without the global entry).
+.ntm/logs/*.log
